channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Unlocking the secrets of an ancient plague
item.description: The first historically recorded pandemic is believed to have struck the walled city of Jirash, in what is now modern-day Jordan, in the 7th century. A new study reveals details about those who died.
item.content:encoded:
The first historically recorded pandemic is believed to have struck the walled city of Jirash, in what is now modern-day Jordan, in the 7th century. A new study reveals details about those who died.

(Image credit: Gatsi)
item.dc:creator: Durrie Bouscaren

item.title: Panic, fury, and some hope, in Iran as U.S. launches strikes
item.description: In Tehran, panicked residents rushed home to shelter and terrified children poured out of classrooms as U.S. air strikes hit the capitol.
item.content:encoded: In Tehran, panicked residents rushed home to shelter and terrified children poured out of classrooms as U.S. air strikes hit the capitol.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: North Carolina Democrats latest to chart future of the party in congressional primary
item.description: In a safe Democratic seat in North Carolina, a match-up between a two-term Congresswoman and a progressive local official show how Democrats are charting the future of their party in the age of Trump.
item.content:encoded:
In a safe Democratic seat in North Carolina, a match-up between a two-term Congresswoman and a progressive local official show how Democrats are charting the future of their party in the age of Trump.

(Image credit: Jonathan Drake/Reuters; Andrew Harnik/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Colin Campbell

item.title: Kyiv's elderly endure blackouts and bombardment, clinging to warmth and hope
item.description: In Kyiv's darkened high-rises, as Russian strikes batter the Ukrainian capital, older residents endure freezing nights and power cuts, relying on volunteers, pets and faith to survive another winter.
item.content:encoded:
In Kyiv's darkened high-rises, as Russian strikes batter the Ukrainian capital, older residents endure freezing nights and power cuts, relying on volunteers, pets and faith to survive another winter.

(Image credit: Eleanor Beardsley)
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley

item.title: Parents, are you sure your kid's car seat is installed right? Here's how to know
item.description: In this visual guide, certified car seat experts walk through common installation mistakes and how to fix them. Learn what a secure car seat base and a tightly fastened tether look like and more.
item.content:encoded: In this visual guide, certified car seat experts walk through common installation mistakes and how to fix them. Learn what a secure car seat base and a tightly fastened tether look like and more.
item.dc:creator: Andee Tagle

item.title: Trump announces 'major combat operations' in Iran
item.description: Israel and the U.S. have launched strikes against Iran, with explosions reported in Tehran and air raid sirens sounding across Israel.
item.content:encoded:
Israel and the U.S. have launched strikes against Iran, with explosions reported in Tehran and air raid sirens sounding across Israel.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Trump says he is 'not happy' with the Iran nuclear talks but indicates he'll give them more time
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said Friday he's "not happy" with the latest talks over Iran's nuclear program but indicated he would give negotiators more time to reach a deal to avert another war in the Middle East.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. President Donald Trump said Friday he's "not happy" with the latest talks over Iran's nuclear program but indicated he would give negotiators more time to reach a deal to avert another war in the Middle East.

(Image credit: Luis M. Alvarez)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Bill Clinton says he 'did nothing wrong' with Epstein as he faced grilling over their relationship
item.description: Former President Bill Clinton told members of Congress on Friday that he "did nothing wrong" in his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein and saw no signs of Epstein's sexual abuse as he faced hours of grilling from lawmakers over his connections to the disgraced financier from more than two decades ago.
item.content:encoded:
Former President Bill Clinton told members of Congress on Friday that he "did nothing wrong" in his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein and saw no signs of Epstein's sexual abuse as he faced hours of grilling from lawmakers over his connections to the disgraced financier from more than two decades ago.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: How the federal government is painting immigrants as criminals on social media
item.description: Experts say this kind of media campaign is unprecedented and paints a distorted picture of immigrants and crime
item.content:encoded: Experts say this kind of media campaign is unprecedented and paints a distorted picture of immigrants and crime
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan

item.title: Pentagon puts Scouts 'on notice' over DEI and girl-centered policies
item.description: After threatening to sever ties with the organization formerly known as the Boy Scouts, Defense Secretary Hegseth announced a 6-month reprieve
item.content:encoded:
After threatening to sever ties with the organization formerly known as the Boy Scouts, Defense Secretary Hegseth announced a 6-month reprieve

(Image credit: X video by @SecWar / screengrab by NPR)
item.dc:creator: Graham Smith
